task_completion.description: Read the free recall data from the specified CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code (e.g., Python using pandas) or a spreadsheet application opening or reading '~/pebl/data/freerecall_data.csv'.

task_completion.description: Calculate primacy, middle, recency, and overall recall scores for participants.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code defining the position bins (primacy: 1-3, middle: 4-12, recency: 13-15) and calculating the mean recall probability for each bin and overall recall per participant.

task_completion.description: Identify and exclude invalid participants based on the specified criteria.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Filtering logic in code checking for overall recall == 0.0 AND mean reaction time < 50ms, or output showing the exclusion of the contaminated participant (e.g., 'sub-999').

task_completion.description: Generate the group serial position curve across all valid participants.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code grouping the data by 'study_position' (1 through 15) and calculating the grand mean recall probability across all valid participants to produce an array of 15 floats.

task_completion.description: Export the final results to the specified JSON report file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code writing a JSON object containing 'participants' (with id, scores, and exclusion status) and 'group_serial_position_curve' to '~/pebl/analysis/serial_position_report.json', or a terminal command confirming the file's creation.

integrity.description: The agent performs actual calculations on the dataset rather than creating a mock JSON file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Execution of a script (e.g., running a Python file or Jupyter notebook cells) that processes the CSV data, rather than just typing out a JSON file manually.

integrity.description: The agent does not hardcode the final serial position curve or participant scores.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code dynamically computes the means and arrays from the loaded dataframe instead of assigning fixed float values to the output variables.

integrity.description: The agent uses the exact file paths specified in the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script explicitly references '~/pebl/data/freerecall_data.csv' for input and '~/pebl/analysis/serial_position_report.json' for output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 30 valid participants and 1 contaminated participant ('sub-999'). The exact contamination criteria to enforce is overall recall of 0.0 and mean response time < 50ms. The output must be saved to /home/ga/pebl/analysis/serial_position_report.json.
